title: tmuxcc session-proxy → client messages
description: All control-plane messages pushed by the session-proxy to a connected client. The session-proxy is the source of truth and pushes state changes; these are read-only events from the client's perspective.

SessionProxyCapabilitiesMessage.description: Session-proxy capabilities advertisement, sent once at handshake time (step 1 of the server-initiates handshake). direction: session-proxy→client.

SnapshotSession.description: The bound session as represented in a snapshot. Only carries identity — the session is always the bound session (always active) on the session-proxy wire.

SnapshotWindow.description: A window as represented in a snapshot.

active.description: True if this is the active window in the session.

synchronizePanes.description: True when synchronize-panes is on for this window at snapshot time. Added tc-7xv.12.

monitorActivity.description: True when monitor-activity is on for this window at snapshot time. Added tc-7xv.15.

monitorSilence.description: Current monitor-silence threshold in seconds, or 0 when disabled. Added tc-7xv.15.

SnapshotPane.description: A pane as represented in a snapshot.

dead.description: True when the pane is dead at snapshot time — a remain-on-exit corpse whose process exited but whose slot survives (tc-4bv2 / tc-295a.10). Additive optional field; absent means false. A dead pane is part of the snapshot so it can be rendered, inspected, and reaped.

exitCode.description: Exit code when dead is true and known (tmux pane_dead_status); absent when alive or unknowable. Additive optional field.

label.description: Durable, driver-owned pane name (tc-1a8z) — the canonical user rename channel, stored in the per-pane @tmuxcc_label tmux user-option. Set ONLY via the rename-pane command (never a title escape), so the shell cannot clobber it; survives a driver restart (re-read from the option on every requery). DISTINCT from the live shell title pane_title (tc-2mn8). Additive optional field; absent/empty means no durable name. Render precedence (durable label > live title > paneId) is the consumer's concern (tc-asyq.6).

bound.description: Durable binding intent (tc-i9aq.1, cold-start.md §4.A) from the per-pane @tmuxcc-bound user-option. True when the user wants a VS Code terminal recreated for this pane on attach. Additive optional field; absent/false means no intent.

detach.description: RESOLVED detach-on-close policy (tc-i9aq.1, cold-start.md §4.A) — the effective first-wins value of @tmuxcc-detach walked pane→window→session. "detach" keeps the tmux pane alive when the tab closes; "kill" exits it. Absent means no scope set a policy (the extension applies its default). Additive optional field.

icon.description: Durable icon policy (tc-i9aq.1, cold-start.md §4.A) from the per-pane @tmuxcc-icon user-option. Absent means no policy. Additive optional field.

paneTitle.description: Live shell window title for this pane — the canonical #{pane_title} value (tc-2mn8; canonical source reworked in tc-s6ov.4). Sourced from a control-mode %* #{pane_title} subscription; catches every title source (shell OSC-0/2, select-pane -T, automatic title from current command). Absent when no title has been observed yet (pane just opened, or shell has not set a title). Empty string means the title was explicitly cleared. Additive optional field.

SnapshotMessage.description: Full-state snapshot, sent once by the session-proxy immediately after the capabilities handshake. Normalized flat arrays; clients build their own in-memory tree by joining on ids. The session-proxy is bound to exactly one session (v3: single-session). direction: session-proxy→client.

windows.description: All windows in the bound session.

focus.description: Currently focused pane/window pair. Both are null if no pane is focused.

attachedClientCount.description: Number of session-proxy-protocol clients connected at snapshot time. Additive optional field (tc-1elae Phase 2). Live updates arrive via ClientCountChangedMessage (tc-44wu0).

connectionId.description: THIS client's own connectionId, assigned by the session-proxy on accept (tc-ozk.2). The client compares it against origin.connectionId on pane.opened / window.added to decide whether a creation is its own. Additive optional field; re-sent on every snapshot (incl. resync).

PaneOpenedMessage.description: A new pane has been created. direction: session-proxy→client.

active.description: True if this pane is the active (focused) pane at the moment of opening.

dead.description: True when this pane is already dead when it enters the model — a remain-on-exit corpse observed on cold attach / reconnect (tc-4bv2 / tc-295a.10). Additive optional field; absent means false.

exitCode.description: Exit code when dead is true and known (tmux pane_dead_status); absent otherwise. Additive optional field.

label.description: Durable, driver-owned pane name (tc-1a8z) when this pane is born / replayed already carrying a @tmuxcc_label user-option (e.g. cold attach to a previously-renamed pane). Additive optional field; absent/empty means no durable name. Subsequent changes arrive via pane.label-changed. See SnapshotPane.label.

origin.description: Causality tag (tc-ozk.2). PRESENT when this pane was created by a wire verb (split-pane / open-window): names the connection + requestId that caused it. ABSENT when foreign (native client, script). Additive optional field; supersedes the bare `created` flag (tc-3y8.2). A client compares origin.connectionId against its own connectionId to decide whether this pane is its own.

bound.description: Durable binding intent (tc-i9aq.1, cold-start.md §4.A) when this pane is born already carrying @tmuxcc-bound — e.g. a cold attach that observes a pane a previous session marked. Absent means no intent. Additive optional field.

detach.description: RESOLVED detach-on-close policy (tc-i9aq.1) when this pane is born carrying a @tmuxcc-detach policy at any scope. Absent means inherit. Additive optional field.

icon.description: Durable icon policy (tc-i9aq.1) when this pane is born carrying @tmuxcc-icon. Absent means no policy. Additive optional field.

PaneLabelChangedMessage.description: A pane's durable, driver-owned name changed (tc-1a8z) — the canonical user rename channel (the per-pane @tmuxcc_label tmux user-option). DISTINCT from the volatile shell title (tc-2mn8): set ONLY via set-option -pt %N @tmuxcc_label, never a title escape, so the shell cannot clobber it. `label` absent means the durable name was CLEARED (empty rename). direction: session-proxy→client.

label.description: The new durable name; absent when the name was cleared (back to unset).

PaneDeadChangedMessage.description: A pane's dead state changed without the pane leaving the session (tc-4bv2 / tc-295a.10 shared pane-state shape). A live pane became a remain-on-exit corpse in place (dead=true), or a dead pane respawned back to live (dead=false). DISTINCT from pane.closed (which means the pane slot left list-panes entirely). direction: session-proxy→client.

dead.description: True when the pane is now dead; false when it returned to live.

exitCode.description: Exit code when dead is true and known (tmux pane_dead_status); absent otherwise.

PaneClosedMessage.description: A pane slot left list-panes entirely (exited fully or was killed). tc-295a.10 strong contract: emitted exactly once per removed slot. DISTINCT from pane.dead-changed (a remain-on-exit corpse that stays in the model). direction: session-proxy→client.

exitCode.description: Exit code of the pane's process, if known. Carried through from a dead corpse's pane_dead_status when the closed pane was previously observed dead; absent when unknowable (non-breaking optional field).

cause.description: Causality tag (tc-u7cu.6). PRESENT when this pane was closed by a wire verb (close-pane / kill-window): names the connection + requestId that caused the close. ABSENT when the close was unsolicited (shell exit, external kill-pane). Additive optional field; mirrors origin on pane.opened.

PaneResizedMessage.description: A pane's dimensions have changed. Distinct from ResizeRequestMessage (client→session-proxy). direction: session-proxy→client.

PaneMovedMessage.description: An existing pane was re-homed into a different window (tc-4gor): same paneId, new windowId. Canonical cause is a detached break-pane. The pane keeps its identity, scrollback and all live state; only its window membership changes. direction: session-proxy→client.

PaneModeChangedMessage.description: A pane entered or left a mode (e.g. copy mode, normal mode). Open-ended mode string for forward compatibility. direction: session-proxy→client.

mode.description: Model-level pane mode. Known values: "normal", "copy", "view". Unknown future modes must be treated as opaque strings.

PaneNotifyMessage.description: An attention/status signal recognised on a pane's raw pty byte stream (tc-76m8.1, user-stories.md S9). The driver scans every attached pane's %output for notification/status escapes (OSC 9, OSC 777, BEL, ConEmu OSC 9;4 progress, OSC 633;D command-exit) and emits one pane.notify per signal, for BOTH bound and unbound panes (the driver is the sole observer of unbound panes). Best-effort passthrough TAP: scanned bytes reach the render path untouched. The extension maps `kind` onto the S9 tiers. `payload` is absent for `bell`, kind-scoped otherwise. direction: session-proxy→client.

kind.description: The signal kind. Tier 1 (interrupting): osc9, bell. Tier 2 (decoration-grade): progress, cmd-exit.

payload.description: Kind-scoped payload. bell: omitted. osc9: message/title/source. progress: progressState/progress. cmd-exit: exitCode.

message.description: osc9: the notification body text (OSC 9 argument, OSC 777 body).

title.description: osc9: notification title, when the source carries one (OSC 777 title;body).

source.description: osc9: which escape carried the notification (diagnostic).

progressState.description: progress: the ConEmu OSC 9;4 progress state.

progress.description: progress: progress percentage 0-100, when the state carries one.

exitCode.description: cmd-exit: the command's reported exit code (OSC 633;D shell integration).

LayoutUpdatedMessage.description: The layout of a window has changed. Clients should apply atomically. direction: session-proxy→client.

FocusChangedMessage.description: The active (focused) pane has changed. If no pane is active, both paneId and windowId are null. direction: session-proxy→client.

WindowAddedMessage.description: A new window was added to the bound session. direction: session-proxy→client.

active.description: True if the new window immediately became the active window.

origin.description: Causality tag (tc-ozk.2). PRESENT when this window was created by a wire verb (open-window, or break-pane's new window): names the connection + requestId that caused it. ABSENT when foreign (native client, script). Additive optional field; supersedes the bare `created` flag (tc-3y8.2).

WindowClosedMessage.description: A window was closed. direction: session-proxy→client.

WindowRenamedMessage.description: A window was renamed. direction: session-proxy→client.

WindowSyncChangedMessage.description: The synchronize-panes state of a window has changed. Added tc-7xv.12. direction: session-proxy→client.

on.description: True when synchronize-panes is on for this window.

WindowMonitorActivityChangedMessage.description: The monitor-activity state of a window has changed. Added tc-7xv.15. direction: session-proxy→client.

on.description: True when monitor-activity is on for this window.

WindowMonitorSilenceChangedMessage.description: The monitor-silence state of a window has changed. Added tc-7xv.15. direction: session-proxy→client.

seconds.description: Silence threshold in seconds (positive = on), or 0 when disabled.

SessionProxySessionRenamedMessage.description: The bound session was renamed. No sessionId field — the bound session is implicit on the session-proxy wire (v3, tc-j9c). direction: session-proxy→client.

ClientCountChangedMessage.description: The number of session-proxy-protocol clients connected to this session has changed. Added tc-44wu0. direction: session-proxy→client.

count.description: Authoritative connected-client count at the moment the event is sent. On connect: includes the newly-connected client (≥1). On disconnect: excludes the just-disconnected client (≥0).

PaneAttachFailedMessage.description: A pane attach targeted a pane the session-proxy does not know about. Fail-loud, named error for the attach path (tc-295a.8). Distinct from command.response failures because pane.attach is not a correlated request. direction: session-proxy→client.

code.description: Error code. Known value: "pane.not-found". Open-ended for forward compatibility.

PaneHydrationBeginMessage.description: Sentinel: per-pane hydration is starting (tc-295a.9). The session-proxy is about to deliver the clear-then-replay hydration frame for paneId on the data plane. Live %output bytes for paneId arriving between begin and end are QUEUED by the driver (no interleave) and replayed after end. direction: session-proxy→client.

PaneHydrationEndMessage.description: Sentinel: per-pane hydration is complete (tc-295a.9). The clear-then-replay frame for paneId has been delivered and any live bytes queued during the hydration window have been flushed. All subsequent data-plane bytes for paneId are live output. direction: session-proxy→client.

SessionProxyCommandOkPayload.description: Successful command result payload. Fields are optional because not every command produces a new entity. tc-ozk.1: for the pane/window-CREATING verbs (split-pane, open-window, break-pane) the session-proxy RETURNS the created entity's ids here — `paneId` is the created pane and `windowId` the window it lives in (recovered from tmux's `split-window`/`new-window`/`break-pane -P -F '#{pane_id} #{window_id}'` reply). The host binds by these returned ids when the pane materialises; no observer/claim correlation is needed. tc-295a.11: `text` carries the captured pane text for pane.capture responses.

info.description: session-proxy.info diagnostics payload (tc-x6l). Present only in session-proxy.info responses.

metricsText.description: Prometheus text exposition of all per-session-proxy counters and histograms.

clients.description: Connected session-proxy-wire clients and the durable identity each presented at handshake (tc-4b6k.1 / D2). One entry per live client connection to this bound session. `identity` is absent for a connection that did not advertise one. Additive optional field — carried for observability; no behavior depends on it in this revision.

stormWindowTotal.description: Total topology events in the current sliding window.

stormWindowBreakdown.description: Per-kind breakdown of events in the current sliding window.

text.description: Captured pane text for pane.capture responses (tc-295a.11 / W3.3). Full UTF-8 scrollback string as tmux emits it: rows separated by bare LF. Present only in pane.capture command responses; absent otherwise.

SessionProxyCommandResponseMessage.description: The session-proxy's response to a SessionProxyCommandRequestMessage. Command-specific failures arrive here as result.ok=false; the separate ErrorMessage is for unsolicited/protocol-level errors. direction: session-proxy→client.

code.description: Error code. See WireErrorCode for known values.

ErrorMessage.description: Unsolicited error pushed by the session-proxy. ONLY for errors not attributable to a specific outstanding command request. For command-specific errors, the session-proxy sends a SessionProxyCommandResponseMessage with result.ok=false. direction: session-proxy→client.

code.description: Error code. Known values: "protocol.unknown-message", "protocol.malformed", "protocol.version-mismatch", "session.unavailable", "internal". Open-ended for forward compatibility.

correlationId.description: If set, ties this error to a prior SessionProxyCommandRequestMessage. Absent for fully unsolicited errors.

cause.description: tc-fah2: for code:"session.unavailable" — the cause of the session ending. "pane-exit" means the session death was the tail of the last bound pane's process exit cascade (user performed the exit); downstream can silence all attribution toasts. "external" means the server/session died without an attributed pane-exit cascade heading it (external teardown, kill-server, etc.); downstream shows the one explanatory toast. Absent for other error codes and for backward compatibility.

PaneTitleChangedMessage.description: The live shell window title of a pane has changed (tc-2mn8, canonical source reworked in tc-s6ov.4). The session-proxy sources the value from a control-mode %* #{pane_title} subscription, catching every title source — shell OSC-0/2, select-pane -T, automatic title from the current command. `title` is the new title string; empty string means the shell cleared it. Consumer precedence: @tmuxcc_label (durable) > pane.title-changed (live) > paneId (fallback). Non-breaking additive delta. direction: session-proxy→client.

title.description: The new live shell window title. Empty string means the shell cleared it.

PanePolicyChangedMessage.description: A pane's durable policy/intent (cold-start.md §4.A) changed (tc-i9aq.1). Carries the per-pane @tmuxcc-* user-options: `bound` (binding intent), `detach` (RESOLVED close policy — first-wins walk of pane→window→session), `icon` (durable icon policy string). Emitted either optimistically (input-path injects internal:set-pane-policy after set-object-policy) or when a requery re-reads new values. `bound` is always present; `detach` and `icon` absent means that option returned to unset. Non-breaking additive delta. direction: session-proxy→client.

bound.description: Binding intent: true when @tmuxcc-bound is set for this pane.

detach.description: RESOLVED detach-on-close policy (first-wins pane→window→session walk of @tmuxcc-detach). Absent when unset at every scope.

icon.description: Durable icon policy string from @tmuxcc-icon. Absent when unset.

SessionProxyMessage.description: Discriminated union of all messages the session-proxy pushes to the client. Narrow on `type`.
